$comment: Layer B selection cases for FREIGHT vs the real GnuCOBOL harness image (COMP-3 value subset + packed output record, docs/comp3.md). The legacy side runs the hex-serializing wrapper: DISPLAY keys (SHIP_ID/CHARGE/TIER) followed by FILEHEX= - the record file as one lowercase hex string, compared as an exact string, so the packed encoding is verified byte-for-byte. Candidate B encodes the signed charge with the unsigned F sign nibble: every DISPLAY key identical, one nibble wrong in FILEHEX on every case - the packed-sign migration bug only the byte-level contract catches. Cases cover a rate-exact charge, both half-cent ties, the BULK tier boundary from both sides, and zero and max weights (zero also pins the zero-sign-normalizes-to-C rule).

legacy.label: COBOL 85 FREIGHT (GnuCOBOL via harness image legacymind/legacy-freight, hex-serializing wrapper)
